title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture
statement_text: For every hereditary property $\mathcal{P}$ which is not satisfied by all graphs, there exists a constant $\varepsilon(\mathcal{P}) > 0$ such that every graph of $n$ vertices with property $\mathcal{P}$ has a clique or an independent set of size at least $n^{\varepsilon(\mathcal{P})}$.
context_text: The Erdős-Hajnal conjecture is described as one of the most challenging open problems in Ramsey theory. The paper investigates whether bounded VC-dimension — a hereditary property shared by most geometrically defined graph classes — explains the exceptionally strong Ramsey-type properties observed in those classes, thereby nearly settling the conjecture for graphs with bounded VC-dimension.
paper_contribution: Proves (Theorem 1.1) that every $n$-vertex graph with VC-dimension at most $d$ contains a clique or an independent set of size at least $e^{(\log n)^{1-o(1)}}$, nearly matching the conjectured polynomial lower bound $n^{\varepsilon(\mathcal{P})}$ and improving the general lower bound $e^{\varepsilon\sqrt{\log n}}$ of Erdős and Hajnal for all hereditary properties.
notes: PDF source — math notation may be garbled in places. Attributed year 1989 inferred from standard bibliographic knowledge of reference [16] (Erdős–Hajnal, Discrete Applied Mathematics). The paper's conclusion section, which may contain additional author-stated questions, was not fully provided in the extracted text.
paper_title: Erdos-Hajnal conjecture for graphs with bounded VC-dimension
